title: sarcotheca diversifolia
intro: Sarcotheca diversifolia, also known as the Philippine or Asiatic grape, is a type of climbing plant native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its purple or yellowish flowers and edible grapes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sarcotheca diversifolia include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions and wounds. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is also consumed fresh or dried as a fruit.
